texto: It is possible, he thought, to be completely happy in marriage--though you must be willing to hold on when your ship was lost at sea and there was no guarantee of rescue.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Adam Ross sobre temas ligados a reflexao. texto: Perhaps it's simply the dual nature of marriage, the proximity of violence and love.

texto: I think that if you have a knack for storytelling, and you work really hard at it, you'll have a chance to tap into something deep. But the fact remains that good sentences are hard won. Any writer worth a lick knows constructing a sentence, a paragraph, or a chapter is hard work.
explicacao: Citação de Adam Ross sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “I think that if you have a knack for storytelling, and you work really hard at it, you'll have a …” articula reflexao, work, hard-work em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como trabalho e hard-work.

texto: ...in response to whatever Alice was struggling with, whatever had caused her to withdraw from him, he had chosen the arms of another woman instead of relying on his own fortitude, as if he'd somehow deserved more comfort than Alice herself had been able to give, or not. Which was part of marriage, after all, part of the vows: enduring those times. And this sense of entitlement seemed to him an even greater sin than infidelity.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Adam Ross: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “...in response to whatever Alice was struggling with, whatever had caused her to withdraw from hi…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, marriage, whatever pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de casamento e termino.

Which was part of marriage, after all, part of the vows: enduring those times. And this sense of entitlement seemed to him an even greater sin than infidelity.
